name: Seghpos Ajemian, Souren
motto: A hard worker and a jolly good fellow withal.
description: Sam started his college career as a farmer, but soon changed his mind and is now taking a real course. He is majoring in Economics and minoring in throat-cutting. Nevertheless his services are in great demand at Schoomaker's Ideal Bowling Alleys and Tonsorial Parlors. Sam is better off than some of us because if he does not succeed in the business world he will still have his barber's trade to fall back upon. Except for a few Friday nights at Lee Hill and an occasional visit to a certain place on the road to "P. F." Sam tends diligently to his studies.

name: Ayre, Perley Fernando
motto: Life is real! Life is earnest!
clubs: President N. H. Y. P. O. (3)
description: We don't know anyone to whom life is more real, or more earnest than to this curly-headed fellow. He's doing something all the time, and between breaths he finds time to direct the business enterprises of the Glee Club, steer the N. H. Y. P. O. in safe and prosperous paths, make several honorary fraternities, and pull about ninety or more in every study, except that of I. We sometimes wonder how he does it. "P. F." was here in the S. A. T. C., but somehow he much prefers to be called a "Tommie" rather than a "Sammie."

name: Bailey, Edson Martin
motto: Ladies and Gentlemen — and Republicans.
clubs: President N. H. Y. P. O. (2)
President N. H. C. Cox and Roosevelt Club
description: "Ed" came to us from the beautiful shores of Lake Sunapee — "God's Country" as he calls it. As a Democratic politician he succeeded last fall in winning the state election for the Republicans. Besides keeping his averages high enough to retain a Valentine Smith scholarship, he has found time to participate in a number of college activities, including a make-up course in "Mil. Art." Seriously though, there is no one in college more willing to work his head off for a cause in which he believes than is Edson. It may be added that he has a way of getting what he goes after. We are watching you "Ed."

name: Bailey, Paul Joseph
motto: Have you got 'em?
description: Bo arrived in Durham with a New York accent and a desire to be a chemist, but due to the fact that he overslept on the morning of a Math. final, the King advised him to take the Arts and Crafts Course. There are two reasons why Bo is not a social butterfly; his musical feet; they're both flat. However, Bo covers a large amount of territory around first base, so after all his suitcases are an asset.

name: Baker, Ula M.
motto: Variety is the spice of Life.
description: Yes, Ula wins everybody's good will, for she is a true sport, a heap of fun and a loyal friend. This saying applies to her in every way: "Ready for fun or ready for work, Ula will never be one to shirk." "Sunshine's" only failing is an incurable liking for Portsmouth. Until lately we always wondered why she spent so many week-ends there, but now we know that "there's a reason."

name: Barker, Ruth Morton
motto: A hard worker and a jolly good fellow withal.
description: The way to a man's heart is through his stomach, so they say, and from all observations we conclude that Ruthie is trying out this theory, judging from her earnest endeavors in Home Economics lines. We have no doubt that she will realize all her desires for we see a strong "Ray" of light in the distance which leads us to that conclusion. In addition to these very commendable domestic tendencies, Ruth has a very amiable disposition which manifests itself in her ever ready smile.

name: Batchelder, William Pickering
motto: Oh how I love to hear those banjos ringing.
description: "Willie": our low string tickler of the four string instrument known as the banjo is one of the manufacturers of genuine saloon and jazz. This boy although not very prominent in college activities excels on the outside; and when asked as to his goings, he says playing; but not what, whereby one might form an opinion of women. This occurred in Haverhill until Batch couldn't use the "stall" of the banjo any longer, or it would have been a permanent contract. But after House Party "Willie" broke the contract and also severed diplomatic relations between himself and a certain young lady. The reason being as "Batch" says, "She doesn't know what House Parties are for anyway." And then they wonder why most of the banjo players are "colored." But in spite of his coloredness we still love our "Willie."

name: Beane, Gladys Helen
motto: For if she will, she will; and you may depend on't; And if she won't, she won't, and that's an end on't.
description: During our first two years here Gladys was a social butterfly but now she looks at college life from a more serious standpoint. Her ambition at present seems to be sharking all history and "poli sci" courses available. Even with all her zeal for obtaining knowledge she spends many anxious moments waiting for that toll call from a popular metropolis nearby. Does that fact solve the mystery that while most people in the stream of life "Dodge" an "Eddy", Gladys bravely faces one?

name: Bishop, Floyd Eugene
motto: If pleasure interferes with studying, cut out the studying.
description: "Bish" may be demoted in the ranks of engineers but he is rapidly being promoted in the Kitchen Scullions Union. From potato peeler at Thomas', he has been promoted to dishwasher. He says the only reason why he doesn't go to informals is that he hasn't the price, but we have our doubts, for we understand that he was seventh choice at a dance once. By his actions on the campus, one would judge that he isn't strong for the women, but if one has any such notion, he ought to notice the pictures over his desk.

name: Berry, Marion Emma
motto: Laugh and the world laughs with you.
description: No matter how sad you may feel, you'll get over it when Babe comes along. Her favorite stunt is cracking jokes to people after they have been asleep for several hours. Besides being a literary fiend and a Zoology shark "Babe" is intensely interested in the subject of "Fogg" and makes it a practice to devote nearly every week-end to its study. We understand, however, that in the future, the study will be carried on through the aid of the U. S. mail service. Excitement — oh, is that a new engagement ring? "A girl who can work, a girl who can play; A girl who's a true friend every day."

name: Benfield, Elvira
motto: It's good to be merry and wise.
description: Benny hates to work but when she does you'd be surprised if you could see her on the hockey field or in dramatics or even in those "snap" organic chem. and bacteriology courses which she elects. Durham saw much of Benny last year but as her "Dot" and her "Ray of Sunshine" are both in Exeter, Durham has little interest for Benny.

name: Blodgett, Randolph Chandler
motto: Unthinking, idle, wild, and young, I laughed, and danced, and talked, and sung.
clubs: Advertising Manager of The New Hampshire (3)
description: Look ye all and gaze upon it! 'Tis none other than our friend Randy. He arrived in Durham just in time to go through the last maneuvers with the College Unit. He is afflicted with the Engineering course; and, marvelous to tell, has actually been known to study (upon state occasions). You can't help liking him because he'll agree with you in anything you say.

name: Boothman, Marion L.
motto: I can't be bothered.
clubs: N. H. Y. P. O. Sec'y. (3)
description: This is the rule upon which Mary Ann has based her life through the first three years of her college career. Whether she ever will be bothered is a question open to debate. The fact that she is a botherer is, however, well established in the minds of her close friends and brought to the fore especially in her work on The New Hampshire and in Cercle Français which she hopes in time to reform to the point where it will make her president. Another object of her reformatory impulses is Tommy, whom she hopes to have fully in hand by the end of the year. The very fact that Mary Ann will go so far as to room with her victim is an index of the amount of that inestimable marceaunian virtue—l'enthusiasme—which she possesses.

name: Boutwell, Llewellyn
motto: Abide a little longer here, And thou shalt take a nobler leave.
description: Red is typically a child of nature, fearless, frank, and free. He is always happy and greets you with a smile. But he refuses to be led into society, even of the gentler sort. In the spring he takes a twirl at baseball. Red has proven that it is much easier to get a high average on the speedometer than in class, but his interest in his bacteriology course proves that he is turning over a new leaf, at least once in a while.

name: Brown, Raymond Chessmin
motto: If there were dreams to sell Do I not know full well What I would buy?
description: This fellow is a dreamer although a Chem. Engineer and he has a mind for something else beside chemical reactions and trinitrobenzenesulphonate of yttrium. In his second year he learned the game of chess and ever since then he has contemplated registering Arts and Science. He is every inch a business man, jumping from Wall Street to Bradstreet's with amazing rapidity. If you want to find him ask Arlo, but if you want to know him approach him with an easy look and let him sell you something.

name: Calpin, Arthur Raymond
description: A few years ago a young man came to Durham to obtain an education and all that goes with it. Due to his affable nature and "one muscle laugh" he became popular with the fair co-eds. Gaining confidence and experience he has widened his field to include the suburbs of Boston as well as Madbury. Between his studies at "Durham Academy," reducing superfluous avoirdupois and growing hair on his head, Wray's time is pretty well taken up. Eppin's favorite expression is "Ew-nice little girl."

name: Came, George Hazelton
description: Abbie is a commuter from Somersworth and is known as the "Gramp." His great ambition is to clean up a fortune at cards, then establish a chemical plant. "Colonel" has quite a rep as a pool player and often shows up the would-be college stars on the billiard table. "Colonel" was the pride of the college unit in the S. A. T. C. and his wit and humor kept the boys in good spirits at all times.

name: Carpenter, Herbert S.
motto: "It's better to be cussed at, than not to be noticed at all."
description: Herbert like all other youths did not naturally take to studies, his first few years with us; whether this was due to his pleasure loving disposition we cannot tell. In addition to the time he did give his studies a great deal of it was spent in the neighboring towns; however this habit of exploring the surrounding hamlets is fast being overcome. For the past few months there has been a decided change in Carp's life—why, we cannot understand unless his new habit of studying at the "Lib" has been noticed. "He cannot hustle through his life, Consumed with vim and pep. He'd rather slowly amble through Enjoying every step."

name: Casillo, Nicholas Richard
motto: Nick, our rival of Thackeray, stepped out of Keene High with the intention of learning English from A to Z and so joined the Arts and Grafters of N. H. C.
description: His daily "beat" is between Smith Hall and the Commons, corroborating the fact that love follows the footsteps of the "Keene" young man. You see, "dot" will be "dot" no matter where you find her and leave it to "Nick" to dash after her. "Nick's" ability of throwing the hammer and discus along with his college training as an expert scribe and editor has enabled him to win wide popularity among his classmates.

name: Chesley, Guy Kenneth
motto: Small but oh my
description: "Doc", the quiet (?) little "chemicer" came here from Rochester with the aspirations of being a "King." Still water runs deep, but if the attractions at Mount Whittier do not get the better of him, he will some day be rewarded for burning the midnight oil and return, the pride of Rochester, with the coveted sheepskin.

name: Christensen, Ernest Waldemar
motto: He's all mine, He's all mine.
clubs: R. O. T. C. Corporal (1), Lt. (2)
description: In the fall of '16 Erne laid away the hook and line and decided to take up a new line of work at N. H. C. He started the "Aggie" course but not being accustomed to scraping his feet before entering a house, he made a quick shift to the Engineering. Waldie is getting used to a Burleigh voice by week end trips to Franklin. Chris has made a reputation on the gridiron and smashed all records for the dashes between "T" Hall and the Crescent. During the World War "Chris" toured Europe as a charter member of the bicycle squad serving his country brilliantly in that capacity.

name: Cook, Albert Spaulding
motto: Cookie started with the class of 1921 but failing to gain distinction with it he decided to try his luck with the class of 1922.
description: All through his college career his one regret has been that N. H. C. does not employ all female instructors. For the past semester "Cookie" has been "ailin" and in consequence he carries a medical laboratory around with him continually. He is now so much better that he thinks nothing of eating ten oyster crackers for supper. Kensington Road and his ukelele take up about all of "Cookie's" time, but he still manages to give History and Shakespeare an occasional "once over." Taken all in all "Cookie" is a conscientious individual and we are betting that he will take his beauty sleep once a week. As far as women are concerned "Cookie" doesn't give a "Dot" for any of them.

name: Cox, Newton
motto: Diseases desperately grown by desperate appliances are relieved.
description: Newt originated in Manchester and condescended to submit himself to a course in Engineering at N. H. C. He has managed to accumulate a little knowledge from time to time but he specializes in "Thermo-Pneumatics." It took him almost three years to break into social society but Miss Bartlett's dancing class accomplished this. Now Newt is considering taking a course in Floriculture as he is especially fond of "Rhododendrons." Among his many achievements he manages to incur an impromptu attack of measles (at the time of finals), attend classes only when convenient, and with the aid of his copartner eliminates all unnecessary studying in the Dorm.

name: Currier, Preston H.
motto: Pret is a rather studious sort of a chap, yet he never fails to be around when anyone is going to pull off anything.
description: In this respect we doubt if he will ever grow up. "Pret" was here in the S. A. T. C. in the college unit and liked Military Art so well that he keeps right on with it. He is deeply interested in his work and lets few things attract his attention. Women and "Pret" are entire strangers, so Smith Park, the College woods, and Hamilton Hall mean little to this prospective engineer.

name: Chase, Helen Evans
motto: Whose heart is warm, and whose laughter jolly, Who likes good times, but keeps clear of folly.
description: First attracted by stirring tales of college life, Helen came here to learn something of the world. She serenely takes life as it comes with a cheerful smile and greeting for everyone. A better than average student, she takes her part in other activities as well. She is athletically inclined, and although she stars in field hockey, tennis seems to be her favorite sport. She has often played at the court "across the way"; and if you wish to awaken a reminiscent smile, just ask her about the time she made a hit playing tennis with a faculty member of "congenial interests." Frank, democratic, humorous and unassuming, she has a host of friends and well-wishers. Here's to her success!

name: Coutchoucas, Constantina
motto: And her sunny locks Hang on her temples, like a golden fleece.
description: Connie has eyes of the big brown variety, and how she can work them! Or, rather, how they do work themselves; for Connie says she has nothing to do with it. And then there's her hair - so fluffy and golden. She has more than common intelligence, and is neither a shark nor a shirk. She has even been known to argue, on occasion. But most of all, surely, most of all, Connie's a good sport.

name: Dame, Norman Carleton
motto: Silence is golden.
description: "Damie" is a little fellow, but he is right there. He hails from the big city of Rochester, and every Saturday he is seen carrying his little traveling bag toward the Railroad Station. We often wonder what the big attraction is, although "Damie" won't mention it, we have our ideas. He has turned over a new leaf since he has become a Junior and has ceased his weekly trips to that little town south of Durham. He probably has his "reason" up in Rochester.

name: Darvill, Frederick John
description: Having gone through prep school with flying colors and grabbing all of the honors in the state of Maine, "Jawn" decided to put New Hampshire College on the map so registered here in the fall of 1918. After he got here he found that it was already on the map so he decided to spend a part of his time "back in Maine." If there is any class that "Jack" has not cut it is Berwick 1-a, 2-b and 3-c. Although "Jack" does not profess to be a disciple of Isaac Walton he does like to be near "brooks."

name: Dawson, Andrew McGrouther
motto: My mother brought me up to be a man not—
description: When "Doggie" was assistant mayor of Methuen, the hamlet was incorporated as a city, but since he took up his residence in Durham, the courts have ruled that it is in reality, a town. At present, "Andy's" time is taken up in the chem. lab. so he has little time to spend with the co-eds. However, when co-eds are initiated into Alpha Chi Sigma, he will have a chance to see more of them. There aren't enough dances in "Durham" so "Doggie" has to attend outside towns to see his widow. In spite of all these frivolities, he is an A1 chemist.

name: Dodge, Charles Frank
motto: What is, must be studied.
description: From out the chemistry his head he thrusts. He can't do what he would, but what he must. The stuff has got him in a cinch— (Consoling thought when he's been on a "bust".) Here's to his work, towards which his efforts bend: "There's no beginning, never will be end." It makes him nutty; hang the chemistry! The problems done? then let them slide, my friend.

name: Doolittle, Irving Warren
motto: The boy with the baby face and the big blue eyes.
clubs: Chairman, Music Committee for Junior Prom
description: "Dooley", our famous ivory tickler, and manufacturer of genuine syncopation, hails from the seaport town of Portsmouth, approximately eighteen miles from Durham. Now no one would ever believe that this innocent looking youth is a rival of Beethoven, were it not for his rehearsals held on some Sunday night parties. This lovable youth would take a chance with Cleopatra were it not for a disastrous defeat that the city of Rochester closed its doors to him and then "Fat" tried a different locality being Haverhill; but alas, not being able to follow suit, Dooley is now turning great attention to that notorious city of Exeter— but one can gradually see that our idol is returning to home, mother, and Durham.

name: Dickinson, Carl Norman
motto: Love — in this wilderness!
description: Behold! a construction man. Gaze upon him! Dicky's slogan is "If I can't climb the tree of knowledge, I'll take an axe and 'hew-it.'" Dick comes from Nashua but all his interests are here in Durham; we doubt if he were ever homesick. He enjoys salt-water bathing as on two occasions he is known to have plowed through the muck down there by the bridge. He can smoke, drink and swear—but he doesn't!! He can eat, sleep and make love—and he does!! But he will walk six miles after a good Musical Comedy.

name: Doran, Robert H.
motto: Quiet service is the mark of true friendship.
description: This quiet, unassuming young man hails from the "Switzerland of America." After a year of preparation at Holy Cross he decided that the "Aggie Course" had its benefits. However, "a wise man always changes his mind" so "Bob" "economically" changed his. Work is "Bob's" favorite pastime while his favorite book is "God's Country and the Woman."

name: Dresser, Donald Sylvester
motto: There's some advantage in being short, but there's more in being tall.
description: The original K. P. and Mandolin Fiend gets down from deck in time to scour some of the flowers from the plates and make for an eight o'clock. He asserts that he's a topnotcher of an engineer but, like all engineers, he raves about the ease of the Aggie course; however Landscape Gardening nearly proved his undoing. He took the labs in this course by surveying the many beauties of Smith Park with a certain blonde.

name: Doyle, Decima Inez
motto: Tho I am always in haste, I am never in a hurry.
description: Decima managed to sustain the wear and tear of commuting for two years, and then, not being able to decide upon how to fulfill both train schedules and social obligations, she decided to be a "dorm" girl. Her talents have a wide range, but she has decided that the greatest benefits are to be derived from Lit. courses to which she is firmly attached. With her delight in orderliness, and her incomparable good humor and desire to help she has well won for herself many friends whom she will never lose.

name: Eastwood, John Edgar
description: John came to N. H. C. in the fall of '20 from "Mass. Aggie" and immediately proved his worth on the freshman football team. Next term he registered with the class of '22. This proves that John knows a good thing when he sees it. Quiet, unassuming, but a fine fellow, and we feel sure he will go far in his line of work.

name: Emerson, Lester Straw
motto: Yes but goldarn it.
description: "Kaloma" came down in a garoon but "Emma" came in a Ford. We often wonder why "Buster" rooms with his Uncle and why he goes home every week end. Some say that "Emma" is subject to home-sickness but we are beginning to believe that it is heart-trouble. If we could get "Buster's" "EI-I-i-i-i-i-i—there" in print it would be worth a million.

name: Flanders, David James
description: Dave blew into Durham from the biggest town in New Hampshire. Having a desire to accomplish big things, since he was from a big town, he decided to take the Chem. course. It was not long, however, before he found out that the Chem. course does not include co-eds. Nevertheless his determination to become a Chemiker has pulled him through and we must give him credit. Dave's immense wardrobe made him very popular with the boys in the house. His likeable personality and occasional sparks of wit, combined with his remarkable musical ability soon made him a favorite with the co-eds. His chief weakness is in telling automobile stories. He has a special model, The Lulu 12, designed for the tired co-ed. It has a perfect body, pleasing lines, a low back, beautiful lamps, and a clutch that is always working. Ask him about it.

name: Ford, Henry Robert
description: At first we thought this little rambling Ford came from Detroit, but we soon found out that he came from the big city of Derry. We thought this Fliver was slow but since he has become a junior we can't keep up with him. For two years he never talked about the fair sex, but since taking "Dancing at 5 P. M." he has become a regular bear with the women. Like all Fords you can tell him by his sound. When he is a half mile away you can hear his rumbling sound going over the ruts of the engineering course.

name: French, Francis Andrew
motto: Never elated when a man's oppressed, Never dejected while another's blest.
clubs: Assistant Advertising Manager, The New Hampshire (3)
description: The College Unit first claimed him as one of their own. Frenchie has a deep bass voice and his most astounding characteristic is his ability to cackle; he sure can "talk like a chicken." When it comes to chasing up ads for The New Hampshire, he is right on the job. The little Construction bug has got him and if he does not become wedded to the Wilton Soda Fountain, upon graduation, we have hopes for his future. Ask him how the freight connections are between Dover and Rockingham.

name: Gadbois, E. Leander
motto: Don't you remember California in September
description: When the Navy dumped out all the aliens Indian Joe was dumped on New Hampshire College. He purchased a pair of moccasins and now plods his way through the Arts and Science division. In football we must give him credit as being one of the best tackles N. H. ever had; but "Gad" didn't think he was indelible to go around the end. At Maine "Gad" set the football world afire demonstrating the "Gadbois" "loaf of bread" method of carrying the ball. Upon graduating a fast freight or a Chalmers bicycle will carry Gad to sunny California where that jeweled frat pin adorns a "crepe-de-chine," there to settle down in a rose covered bungalow. We wish him happiness and success.

name: Gale, Shirley Irving
motto: O light that lies in woman's eyes, And lies, — and lies, — and lies.
clubs: Friendship Council Y. M. C. A. (2)
First Lieutenant R. O. T. C. (3)
description: This would-be chemist and student of the gyroscope as applied to whirling plates at the Commons early fell for the restless sex and is still falling. His ability in the contrivance of labor saving devices to prevent early rising is known to all. His chief victim is the Palace furnace to which he gives loving attention. Here's wishing you luck, Shirley, and may you come through a winner in the race of life.

name: Gadd, Thomas Burden
motto: Still waters run deep
description: Tom has been noted for slinging hash and serving coffee at the Commons ever since its inauguration. He has served under "Big Bertha's eagle eye" but "the quickness of his hand deceived her eagle eye" so Tom grazed to his content on pie and cream from the top of the College milk before serving the rest to the patrons on the other side of the counter. Tom may appear bashful but you've heard "You'd be surprised."

name: Gay, Paul Baxter
motto: But the time shall come when I shall say that I have no pleasure in them.
description: "P. B." came from New London with straw in his boots and hay chaff over his ears and registered in the 2-year course but finally decided that four years is not too long to be near Boston. Now he receives pink envelopes and dainty sweets daily, but, truly, these cannot all come from one source. Only yesterday, he opened a box of dainties and one of his roommates picked up the following enclosed note. "To my dearest. You can see how well I am progressing now." And we all hope that she keeps up the pace for such things surely tickle our palates. When college days are over, Paul had better take to the tall and unknown timber, where life is free and days of judgment come not.

name: Gilmore, Mildred Eudora
motto: And grace that won who saw to wish her stay.
description: Listen, my children and you shall hear Of that famous storm, one night last year And its results for Mildred, who seems so quiet, And who, although she professes to be "on diet," Took advantage of the B. and M.'s wreck And still with "eaton" was on deck Yet here's to Mildred, a real true friend, Who'll near you stand to the very end.

name: Graham, Edward Dewey
motto: After sharking all the courses at Portsmouth High and deciding that the B. & M. could no longer pay enough for his services at the toll bridge, Dewey decided to come to New Hampshire.
description: His good natured disposition and winning personality, coupled with a wonderful sense of humor soon made him a favorite. His first roommate almost ruined his future, but Dad soon fixed that up for him and since then Dewey has never had to wonder whether or not he was rooming in Smith Hall or in Fairchild. His success on the football field has been remarkable and we do not hesitate to say that he ranks as one of the best guards that New Hampshire has ever had.

name: Griffiths, Sadie Marion
motto: B'ess her heart, dah bebby.
description: Bricks, Cupids, Princes, Kings and everything—no wonder that "Peg" looks forward to trips to Worcester. Sadie Marion is really a very quiet and dignified young lady—but what about "Peg?" Just follow one of the many couples that stroll out the Mill Road every Sunday afternoon and see what the attraction is, in that large house that is, fortunately, within walking limits. We wonder how long it will be before "Peg" takes possession of that furnished house in Cuba. We hope she will wait for a year for we would miss her—good sport and true friend that she is.

name: Goldsmith, William Francis
motto: Oh, how I hate to get up in the morning.
description: "Goldy" decided that Worcester Tech wasn't fast enough so he came to New Hampshire. After his arrival he proceeded to show the boys a few things about the chem. course, and is now firmly started on his career as a "chemicker." He has one failing and that is picking on the diminutive Mayor of Sanbornville. "Goldy" must have had enough of the women in Worcester because he certainly lets them alone here.

name: Gould, John Enshaw
motto: Jack be nimble Jack be quick, Jack's always up to some new trick.
description: Not many years ago Jack hove into Durham to begin dodging the Sophs. The army Lieuts. found him and he became a soldier. When barred from the Home Eco. course he became a member of the night crew at Dube's cafe. John's motto is: "There is so much that is bad in the best of us, That it doesn't behoove any of us to trust the rest of us." In spite of his trusty motto he is always willing to lend anything from advice to a tooth-brush.

name: Hamblet, Theodore Clement
motto: The damn thing moves.
description: Back in 1917 "Teddy" puffed into Durham, gave T-Hall the once over, found his room and took a nap before registering. Ted has always had a wholesome respect for the elements and rain or shine he can usually be seen with a "PARASOL" hooked on his arm. Ted's baby face, blue eyes, curly hair, rosy cheeks and lovable "lithp" are among his many assets. He sure wields a mean slip-stick having broken two on his room mates. Here's the best of luck to the future mayor of Lawrence.

motto: Early to bed and early to rise, Makes her healthy, wealthy and wise.
description: When she was a freshman Down in Bickford Hall, Often midst the noisy cries You could hear her fiercely call. Though she went away and taught, No great change in her was wrought. And in spite of all we say, She's a good scout, anyway.

name: Harmon, Russell S.
motto:
The time I've lost in wooing,
In watching and pursuing
The light, that lies
In woman's eyes,
Has been my heart's undoing.
description: When "Russ" made his appearance at New Hampshire College he had a mandolin in one hand and three little fairies clinging to the other. "Russ" spent two years trying to get an education at Dartmouth and Boston University, after which he decided to take up his studies here. "Russ" has acquired the art of wielding the pencil and is fast becoming a rival to James Montgomery Flagg and Howard Chandler Christy. Besides being an artist, he is a real genius at letter writing and intends to put before the public a book of his works entitled "Dear Hazel."

name: Hatch, Wallace Everett
motto: If you can't be good, be careful.
description: Wally came to New Hampshire from the little hamlet of Kennebunk in the fall of 1916, but the war hindered his college career for a while and Wally saw the other side of the Atlantic, first as a gob, later as a marine, and still later as a gob again. Returning to college two years late, full of ambition and none the worse for his experiences he picked the hardest studies he could find and is now well along in the pre-medic course, with high hopes of some day being a physician. As a result of his interest in Professor Jackson's Zoo Lab. Wally is always blamed when a pet cat is missing from the neighborhood.

name: Holmes, Byron Lucy
motto: Perseverance is the secret of Success.
description: "Homsie" came down here in 1917 determined to run away with all the rugged aggie courses, but some of our wise and learned profs didn't think so; and "Homsie" was forced to slacken his speed. However, reverses didn't conquer his spirit, and there's now no doubt but that he'll come out on top in the end. When Byron wakes up in the morning his chief difficulty is to decide whether his is Myron or Byron.

name: Higgins, Martha Gorham
motto: "Let us, then, be what we are, and speak what we think, and in all things keep ourselves loyal to truth, and the sacred professions of friendship." —also— "With learned mien she burns the midnight kerosene."
description: Martha is small, curly-haired, bright-eyed and sincere. Some say she's reserved, but what of that? 'Tis only the sign of a better friend when once you know her. She passed her sophomore hiking and tobogganing course, which has since developed into a correspondence course, cum laude, and now smiles serenely around her on other struggling sophomores.

name: Howard, Hubbard Everett
motto: "Hib started in college in the fall of '15 but in '17 he heard the call of Uncle Sam and went with the colors to France. He came back to us in '19 and took up his work with the class of '22. Even though he sailed the briny deep 'Hib' never could forget the murmuring of the brooks. His qualities as a soldier reflect his qualities as a man, and we find him dependable, sympathetic, true, and a friend worth while."

name: Hyde, Rhoda Alettha
motto: "Oh, I like to get up in the morning but, I'd rather stay abed."
description: A freshman on his first visit to the cafeteria innocently inquired, "Is that an automatic cash register?" Rhoda, may be too small to be seen, but she is not too small to count with those who know her best. Perseverance is one of her fine qualities as is shown by her persistent struggle with her beloved subject, chemistry. She loves to be practical—witness the course she is taking. None the less she is faithful to other interests, music, art, and poetry.

name: Ingham, Howard Vincent
description: Three years ago a suitcase came to town dragging Howard reluctantly behind. The mud was deep, much to the disgust of Howard so he soon hit the road to Newmarket. He soon got over this habit and may be seen walking towards Broth Hill most any day in the week. He is a regular sponge when it comes to Physics and only sharks the Aggie courses. For all this Howard has a sunny nature and is always ready for a good time even though he has the "Wiley's" now and then.

name: Jacques, Laura Gertrude
motto: Every why has a wherefore.
description: Just to see Laura you would know that she has a mind of her own and Dean French is sure of it. She is not frivolous—that is at all times—and she is never a clinging vine. Has "zoo" or athletics the more interest for her? Perhaps, if Laura did not spend so much time at Jackson's, we could decide. If you want a secret kept, tell it to Laura. Her determination and her capability will surely bring her success in whatever she does.

name: Jenness, Lyle C.
motto: Little but oh my!
description: "How large the world is!" These were the words ejaculated by this diminutive cohort enlisted from "Way Back." When the war came on Uncle Sam proclaimed him too young to fight, so he returned broken hearted, to tamp ties on the B. & M. Not to be beaten by circumstance he returned in the fall to tread the trail of his brother, this time more literally than before, for he took his course, his Ford, and his girl, not to mention his scholastic prestige. Can you beat it!

name: Johnson, Frederic Knowlton
motto: Nobody loves a blond.
clubs: Managing Editor of Y. M. C. A. Handbook
description: It is said that tow-headed Swedes make good penny-ante players, but "Swede" has always proven an exception to the rule. However, he is very generous for he gladly contributes to the cause, and charges his loss to the fact that he is not a psychologist. "Jack" is also somewhat of a mechanician, and spends a great deal of time in studying autos. He has found that when riding it is better to put the top down, because it makes the ride more (Arey). He has a strong personality, and pull (?), as is seen by his marks in certain subjects, but we are sure that he will succeed in the end.

name: Kavanaugh, Edward Charles
description: This handsome young man claims he has numerous suitors but when quizzed concerning same says that he always takes his sister to the movies. Do brothers as a rule take their sisters to the movies? Others' sisters by the looks, Eddie. Eddie is a Commuter and loves eight o'clock classes for which he is obliged to take the 5:57 train from Somersworth. Oh, how I hate to get up in the morning. Cheer up, Ed, when the Durham electrics start you can have a special car, and leave an hour later. George and Mike the original Trolley kids.

name: Kelsey, Howard Phelps
motto: If it were done, when 'tis done, Then it were well it were done quickly
description: "Kellie's" chief disappointment in his Junior year was the lack of dog parties. You could always hear him advocating a dog party. However, for this disappointment he made up in many various ways, among them, in patronizing Old Uncle Sam's mails in spite of Burleson and Probation. Because of a poetical soul, "Kellie" is well versed in the language of the Gods, you always find him at your right hand for any fun.

name: Leonard, Janice Marjorie
motto: One moment sunshine, two of trouble, Will we ever find her double? Exam time is the time she worries, And packing trunks is when she hurries. Fair Cupid has played quite a part, For he 'Pierce'd' Janice with his dart. Just when this happened we can't tell, We think 'twas in a padded cell. She does not mind the mud at all, And often walks to Packers' Falls. As an artist she's won fame And she's a good sport just the same.

name: Lewis, Frank Herbert
motto: Mary's little Lamb
description: This ambitious youth has had a wild and wooly career. After "prepping" at Lowell Textile he decided to "Join the Navy to See the World." And he saw it, too. He'll tell you so. Spain, Turkey and Gibraltar were only week-end trips for the U. S. S. Arizona. Seafaring was too tame for Frank so he thought he'd try the woods. Consequently he entered N. H. as a Forester. Frank is a student, too. Burns midnight oil and gets the marks that go with it. He specializes, however, and has won the attendance cup at Smith Hall!

name: Lowe, Carroll Henry
motto: The derivative of a man is a minus sign under a radical.
description: "Hi is a grind. He is also an 'Arts Chemiker'. For five years he did not enter the library until he wanted to drop a subject and had to see the Dean. Then he hired a guide. He also has the reputation of being the one man in college who has never been in Smith Hall. When they built Hamilton Hall 'Hi' thought it was a new 'gym.' The nearest he ever comes to being a social butterfly is playing in the orchestra for informals. He thought seriously of dropping Education because of implied fairs and church bazaars. He is, however, a 'wiz' on the cornet, and a steady dependable friend."

name: Lyster, Paul John
motto: A friend e'er tried and true.
description: To all that know him that is just what "P. J." is. He is one swell fellow and that is all that can readily be said about him. If there is anything to be done from pulling the "Freshies" across the pond (which he didn't do) to attending a dog party he is right on hand. He even said he liked going to Chapel - because he had to. He enjoyed Military Art so much that he took part of it over again. We are sure that "P. J." will make a good farmer and a splendid citizen.

name: Mckelvie, Carl Donald
motto: He never knows when he has enough.
clubs: Second Lieutenant R. O. T. C. (2)
description: This original wild man has duly been crowned "King of Fairyland," after having been passed the "Buck" by his predecessor. He tried the rough and rocky paths of an engineer, but now his chief ambition is to become an Expert Accountant. However, the only part of this title he has earned yet is the Ex. which stands for "Extra Noisy". "Buck has relatives far and near, the nearest is his faithful sister in Rochester, but a more dear one, 'mid the hills of Caroline."

name: Marsh, Robert French
motto: Variety is the Spice of Life
description: After receiving his diploma from Manchester High "Bob" thought he would like to increase his knowledge along "chemical lines." Contrary to the expectations of several he is still sticking to the "chem" course, and judging by his marks he must either fit with the "King," or else he is showing the boys what a man reared in "Manchester" and "Concord" can do. During his Junior year "Bob" joined the staff of "Grant's Emporium" and established an enviable record there. Except for an occasional visit to the "Capital City," or "Newmarket," "Bob" lives in the Chem Lab. and tends diligently to his studies.

name: Mattoon, Donald Potter
motto: Sleep and the World sleeps with you, But if you arise at four, you arise alone.
description: "Beke's" first great achievement at N. H. C. was the proper manipulation of the "Black Maria," the one-lunged milk truck of the dairy department. "Don" formerly occupied the front row at all the roughhouses but since his nose interfered with the feet flying around, he has taken a back seat. No doubt, he fits with the chickens for he fed them, formerly, over the counter at the cafeteria and now is feeding them out of his hand at the poultry plant.

name: Marshall, Nathalie Emerson
motto: 'Tis well to be merry and wise, 'Tis well to be honest and true; 'Tis well to be off with the old love Before you are on with the new.
description: Now "Nat" is all of these, merry and wise, honest and true and, as for new, well, men to her are "creatures of circumstance" and just what the circumstances are does not matter one whit to her. You should see her at an informal, with special emphasis on the "in," or at a New Year's celebration, or under the starlit skies, or in the movies where she never fails to fall in love with the leading man. As for the men, why they simply agree that "A little peach in an orchard grew," But we love her just the same.

name: Marshall, Leonard Joseph
motto: She used to be so shy and bashful—but you ought to see her now.
description: Rave on Macbeth; Jo and his little song are never separated. He is contented to remain in Durham as long as he gets his three letters (feminine) a week and can spend a few week ends in Salem. Say "Gertrude" and watch him jump. Jo believes thoroughly in M. E. and M. E. believes thoroughly in him. Aside from his regular prescribed course of studies, Jo considered it quite essential that he learn the "light fantastic steps," thereupon he subjected himself unto Miss Bartlett's care. The course "took;" he has not been the same since.

name: Marden, Marguerite Elizabeth
motto: The reason firm, the temperate will, Endurance, foresight, strength and skill, A perfect woman, nobly planned.
description: And who, pray tell, is this very short girl who draws short pictures on account of her height and speaks in a soft, sonorous whisper? Oh, don't you know? That's Marge who hails from the banks of the Hudson. Marge has that constant habit of awakening everyone at early hours, and refusing to lie in bed. However, this is nothing serious for the early bird is always the winner. This means she's square.

name: Adams, Martin
motto: Why won't you now? You always used to.
clubs: Assistant Business Manager of the New Hampshire (2), (3)
Senior Captain R. O. T. C. (3)
description: "Crab" comes from Pembroke, 'nuf said. Does Crab believe in Co-education? Ask "the Deacon's Second Wife," she knows. Crab is a busy, hard working man but he has a "K" in Smith Hall 24 (a) (b) (c). Now Crab wonders if he can pass the make up with an "A". But when it comes to baseball "Crab" not only gets his "K"s (strike outs), "A"s (assists) on the diamond, but he also has a "K", "A" in the bleachers.

name: Mayo, Irene S.
motto: A sunny temper gilds the edges of life's blackest cloud.
description: The latest addition to our class came when Irene landed in Durham last Fall. She had wandered to our metropolis from way up in the north country, Montreal, Canada. Anywhere and anytime she greets you with a cheery "hi" and that ever present smile. Why she is always happy and even in "exam" time when some of the co-eds have Moody spells, Irene smiles on. Although "Speed" seems to have no great liking for the males of Durham she haunts the post office anxiously waiting for letters to arrive from foreign shores. We wonder if there is any connection between those overseas notes and the fact that Irene is learning the fine arts of cooking in her spare moments. Anyway she is all right, and we wish her the best of luck in all her enterprises.

name: Stillman, Ernest McKerley
motto: Gee — but I love my girl!
description: The county farm whistle blew, the crowd gathered at the station, while the town band played "Good-bye Papa's Shekels" and another son of Gerrish boarded the "Busted and Mangled" for "Durham" college. During "Mac's" first year he got away with a stiff course in letter writing. While a sophomore he spent the greater part of his time practicing on his ukelele and many a night tears have been brought to his brothers' eyes when they heard him play "Memories".

name: McNulty, Elizabeth Mary
motto: They win that laugh.
description: If you love to do things on the spur of the moment — and most unexpected things too — pal with Betty. She's full of fun and generous too — if not, why the large tips left on her trays at the Commons? Betty keeps us worried as to what she will do next because once she jumped an express in Dover — locals to Durham are too slow! Durham used to bore Betty, especially week-ends, and Haverhill was the town, but now things have changed. Betty really has a serious side too, expressed in essays for Dr. Richards and by social work in Dover. In fact social work is her hobby.

name: Meader, Walter Sidney Jr.
description: How this youth came to be called Tucker is yet a mystery. Tucker is a shark and not a drone, a woman hater but not a girl hater for his Dover admirers are mostly in the kindergarten. Time was when Tucker wore out the marble rail on Lothrops & Pinkham's soda fountain. How he blushes when reminded of this. Tucker is a desperate man; he visits Central Park and Somersworth often in search of excitement. On one of these expeditions Tucker excited a policeman into the firing of his .77 automatic and was haled into court. As for the Park 6 — ask Mike.

name: Moriarty, James Joseph
motto: They can all be tamed.
description: In his freshman year Jim was as meek as could be, but as time passed he developed. He is now taking both of those courses known as Dover, 21c and Newmarket, 2 a.m. Be careful, Jim, for the pitfalls on that short but happy road are many. Nevertheless Jim has a heart as large as "Pettee Block" and he is full of fun. Where he is there is sure to be plenty of witty sayings and a general good time. If he could only forget Fitchburg he would be all that any girl could desire.

name: Morrison, Clifton Cole
motto: There was a sound of revelry by night.
clubs: Vice-President N. H. C. Tennis Association
description: —and there sure was if Junk was anywhere around. He has a habit of dropping into one's room and saying, "Well, — if anyone has anything to say about it." One night he favored us with a selection regarding the dreary spring weather. Ever since then he has contemplated Grand Opera rather than the more practical enterprises of a construction engineer. We would not be the ones to discourage him, for alas, poor Yorick, this life is but a vale of tears, and who wants to wreck a promising young man.

name: Newman, Derwood Austin
motto: Aim high and you won't fall short.
description: Danny arrived here with the high ambition of becoming a chemist and in spite of several interruptions he is soon to attain his goal. His musical ability was first apparent when he rendered violin selections for the inmates of Smith Hall. This may be the reason for his popularity with the fair sex. But it is rumored he is doing a little research in this line over in York State. "Who knows?" Upon his return to N. H. C. the army lost a good aviator and Dr. Slobin has another worry. But then "math" isn't "chem" anyway.

name: O'Leary, Michael Francis
motto: The wild, wild women,—they're making a wild man of me.
clubs: First Lieutenant R. O. T. C. (2)
description: Behold! the pride of Portsmouth (self-appointed). Mike holds the record in his home town as a modern Romeo. At the mere mention of his name—sighs can be heard from all directions. Mike's motto is to get them young, treat them rough, and tell them nothing. But, like Anthony of old, he loved and lost. Mike, however, has distinguished himself as a scholar, rising like a meteor above the common swine. He dreams of mahogany furniture and doors of frosted glass bearing the inscription Mr. O'Leary, C. P. A.

name: Olsson, Gunnar Bror
motto: Gold does not always glitter.
description: Oleo came to N. H. C. from the backwoods of Chichester with hopes of becoming a great man in the Aggie World. The prospects are still good for him to achieve his aim. During the first part of his career he was tenderly cared for by Big Bertha of Mess Hall fame. The evidences of her influence can be seen today as he still follows her teachings and at present is cashier at the Commons. Although he is not what is termed a social butterfly, at times his behavior is suspicious.

Gold does not always glitter.

name: Perkins, Perley Chesman
motto: I have immortal longings in me.
clubs: Treasurer Parnell-Corriveau Post 385 V. F. W. (2), (3)
description: Since becoming the secretary of various organizations, "Perk" has acquired the knack of taking notes briefly. He has become so proficient in this art that he has just sent to the press a simplified edition on "Methods of Making Love." "Perk" has surely made good since coming to New Hampshire and we promise a very bright future for him in literary lines after getting his sheepskin from N. H. C.

name: Plummer, Raymond S.
description: Raymond blew in from the wilds of Laconia bent on engineering, but he soon found that engineering constituted everything but extended courses in Exeter and Dover. As an economist Ray sure is a winner for there are few who can boast of such a wardrobe; how he does it we would like to know. Traveling seems to be his hobby although we confess that he would like to become a successful dance promoter. How about it, Ray? When it comes to trying out for Manager, Ray sure is a sticker.

name: Proctor, Ralph Harwood
motto: To sigh, yet feel no pain, To weep, yet scarce know why: To spend an hour with Beauty's chain, Then throw it idly by.
description: This six-footer has subjected himself to a thorough course in E. E. and from all present appearances we have great hopes that he will lay hands on that sheepskin next year. As a social butterfly Ralph sure makes a hit with the fair sex; it must be his wonderful blush that attracts them. He believes that "variety is the spice of life" so he has no "steady." Just mention the word horse and Ralph will talk for a whole evening. We wonder why it was that he only attended one dancing class.

name: Reardon, Charles Edward
motto: Let me but do my work from day to day and I will be happy.
description: "Charlie" comes to us from a spot in New Hampshire known as Concord to follow in the footsteps of his brothers who have gone before. He transferred to the University of Maine in the fall of 1918, but returned to New Hampshire when he learned that a "Secretarial" course was now being offered for the first time. He took to this course very strenuously but as competition was very keen he flunked out. But anyway Charlie wanted to tackle bigger things. Charlie served valiantly during the World War as an embryo officer. He managed to reach Camp Lee, Virginia, fifteen minutes before the signing of the armistice.

name: Rossell, Joseph Frederick
motto: Even though vanquished he could argue still
description: In the fall of 1918, (Freddie), a big, brawny "Swede" from the Capitol City, arrived in Durham, and took up his residence in Pettee Hall. Not being accustomed to such high living, he moved to Fairchild Hall. He has always worthily and capably upheld the merits of the Arts and Science Course, and no doubt will make a good soap box orator. "Freddie" received his H. S. (hash slinging) degree in Grant's Palm Garden during his Sophomore year, and graduated with honors. During the football season of 1920, "Freddie" accompanied by two other tramps, made a memorable trip to Worcester to see the "Holy Cross" game, returning with an overgrown knee. "Freddie's" ambition is to be a "Coal Baron."

name: Rowell, Richard Irving
motto: Going, going, gone, Too late.
description: Lefty blew into Durham from the Northern wilds with a bar of Hershey's in one hand and a bottle of Herpicide in the other. We discovered Lefty's "Reilly" nature when he met his Concord rival at the station. Lefty spends most of his time in the chem. lab, working on a tonic that will grow hair on a billiard ball, but from appearances he will have to take a Post Graduate course. Nevertheless he is a shark in his studies, a good mixer, and a clever performer on the athletic field.

name: Rumazza, Edward F.
motto: By his sunny smile you will know him.
description: In spite of the fact that he hails from Rochester, Rummy is the original man of wonders. Before taking the platform in behalf of Woman Suffrage he occasionally assisted at Miss Bartlett's dancing classes. He bids fair to make a lasting name for himself in athletics. When not in consultation with the business men of his native city he energetically pursues the engineering course. It is rumored that Eddie shakes a mean leg even to the extent of migrating to Lee Hill and Newmarket.

name: Russell, Bruce E.
motto: I am a walking encyclopedia.
description: "Rus" believes in getting his lessons and as a result his marks show it. During the first two years he held his own with the rough-housing bunch on the lower floor of Fairchild but since this gang has separated, he has quieted down considerably. Travelling as a hobo is his specialty. How about those week-end excursions to Providence, Orono and Worcester as a loyal rooter for the football team? Yes, he's a different boy out of town. His accommodating ways have won him many friends.

name: Saunders, Arthur Bruce
motto: Work is an old fashioned way of getting a living.
description: Homer once told him, "You missed your calling— you should have been a sledge-hammer mechanic;" but all the time he thought he was a chemist. "Abie" has a great avidity for learning. He even poured (water) over the entire chemical library. "Hem's" would-be hobby is chess but he failed miserably at that sport because the "bishops" made him homesick. Even the allurements of a house dance could not dissuade him from going home to review his "diocese."

name: Sawtelle, Eleanor Prescott
motto: Nor do I know what is become Of him, more than the Pope of Rome.
clubs: Delegate to annual Y. W. C. A. Conference in New York
Undergraduate representative of Y. W. C. A. (3)
description: No wonder Eleanor is busy— she has to be to keep all the organizations going and have time for a few classes. Whether it is shooting baskets or leading a Y. W. C. A. meeting, she is always proficient, and if she can't see the point to any jokes on herself, she can always see them if they are on her roommates. But cheer up El— even if we all haven't red hair and the cares of a Child, we still have our troubles. If you ever do decide what you want to do you will succeed.

name: Scott, Elmer Arthur
motto: Little — but oh my!
description: This young lad came to our Granite State College from Tiverton, Rhode Island with the intentions of becoming one of the great engineers of the country, and being able to fill the shoes of Thomas Edison. "Scotty" found that the early life of an engineer was not merely a "slip stick" which the engineers call "Little Joe;" so "Roiden" then put in an application for the Arts and Crafts and was unanimously voted in. From what you see of this ambitious looking urchin, you would be inclined to think that his leisure hours were occupied only in writing for B. and M. passes and collecting laundry. However we are sorry to say that he has a very acute liking for the game of "Hearts"; but, since Scotty lost out in his last love affair, he now believes this to be the only way to win them.

name: Sherwood, Irving Frederick
motto: I'll shoot you a game of pool.
description: To look at "Skim" one would think he was a senator's son or an ambassador or a prize fighter and one is always surprised to learn that he is an "Arts and Crafter" instead of an "aggie" student. Besides reading German for amusement and solving Cost Accounting problems, "Skim's" one pastime is chasing the frolicsome one ball with his favorite cue. Women and "Skim" were strangers until once upon a time a little girl came to live across the way. Since then he has never been the same.

name: Sleeper, Lora
motto:
Oh, the world is wide and the world is grand,
And there is little or nothing new.
But the sweetest thing is the grip of the hand
Of a friend that is tried and true.
description: Lora hails from the Capitol city of our dear old granite state. If there is any work to be done you can always depend upon Lora to do her share. We wonder if the instruction trip to Boston has been the cause of her renewed interest in the Home Economics course. We also wonder if her love for "golden tints" has been gained wholly from books or otherwise (?)

motto: They go wild, simply wild over me.
clubs: President of I. O. O. G. (2), (3)
description: "Swede" started with the class of '21 but on account of the war and other things he slipped a cog and decided to cast his lot with us. Since then his time has been divided between the "Widows' Condolence Club," the Edison Phonograph Co., and New Hampshire State College. He strayed from our fold in the fall of 1919 and registered at Boston University where he stayed just long enough to make one recitation, "On der vay ouvidt".

name: Spinney, Fannie Mae
motto: Maidens should be mild and meek, Swift to hear and slow to speak.
clubs: Delegate to Des Moines Student Volunteer Convention (2)
description: Fannie is one of our most all round girls, no matter whether it's athletics, dramatics, study or fun. Some people may not think Fannie a social butterfly but if you wish to see her blush ask her "where she learned to shimmie." Her ready smile and her cheerful "Hi!" make her a popular girl and her ready hand makes her the best of friends.

name: Smith, Charles Wesley
description: "Wes" drifted up with the tide from Portsmouth and has been drifting ever since. Smithy is so ambitious that he would starve to death if he had to wait on himself. Studies are never one of Smithy's handicaps; he manages to stay in college, but gives no reason for staying. His rating as a chauffeur is a minus quantity since he wrecked the family flivver. Smithy's real athletic ability is latent until spring, when he shines as a catcher on the ball diamond.

name: Stillman, Gay Stanley
motto: Dog-gone the women.
description: Stillman strolled down from New London and Company H introduced him to our glorious Educational Institution. That's all we know. Of his origin and early existence we have never heard. In fact we are beginning to believe that he never had any. One thing that we have learned is that Stillman is a worker. He has stuck to the old M. E. Course for three years and he is still ambitious. Remarkable!! He sharks all the courses with the Dean and Pa Finley, and wonders why he can't do the same under Brainerd.

name: Stevens, Hope
motto: Sayings is one thing; doing another.
description: Hope's mind is a reservoir of English poetry, from which she draws just the right quotation for every occasion. The recent rapid growth of the English department may be attributed to her demand for new courses in this, her favorite subject. Her romance she keeps to herself, but really, it's not because of a roving disposition that she goes to Brentwood every week. As for wit, she can convulse her roommates without showing a trace of a smile herself. While we have Hope, life's worth the living.

name: Storey, Edward Jerome
motto: Although he claims to be one of the Coach's rubbers, he may grow to be an overshoe some day.
description: Here we have the little boy from Sanbornville, N. H. Although little, he can make a big noise. And don't forget that the good things often come in very small packages. If you doubt this, why travel over to Portsmouth on some Tuesday night and watch him when Ferdinando comes to town. Lee Hill also seems to have some attractions in store for him as he is often seen in those gilded haunts in search of pleasure. He is a typical engineer of the five foot type. "He used to be so meek and quiet, but you ought to see him now."

name: Struthers, Francis William
description: After spending a year at N. H. C. answered Uncle Sam's call to see the world through a "port hole", and tickled a wireless operator's key for two years. With many wild tales of the South Sea Isles he once more turned his attention toward further education. He is now sharking Sociology, frying doughnuts, and teaching the boys how to dance the original Hula Hula.

name: Swain, Charles Andrews
motto: On with the dance—let joy be unconfined.
description: Where the women are—Charlie isn't! An essay by him upon the subject of "Women and their Disadvantages", would be worth a fortune. He's taking one Engineering subject but as for the Aggies he says, "I can't give them any time." Every term he asks that a new course in free-hand drawing be offered as he has taken all that are given. He is a great frequenter of Dover, especially of the "Midnight Reviews." As an Arts man we feel sure that he would make a better Artist. We wonder where Charlie gets his vocabulary; as he chews his ice-cream it is very likely that he has swallowed the dictionary.

name: Swasey, Mildred
motto: I am caught by her berry-brown hair, And the rose on her cheek is my joy!
description: To the faculty and to her admiring freshmen friends; seems inapproachable and taciturn, but those who know her best realize that when she does speak, it pays to listen. Her brilliance is not all on the outside for her wit is ready with an answer to every challenge. Those brown eyes bespeak a lovable disposition and an unswerving loyalty to all she holds dear. The question is: How does she always find time to get her studies prepared, and still read all her Bowdoin mail?

name: Taylor, Millard Renshaw
description: In order to keep the water out of the cellar this young drone has to sit up all evening to plug. He is a commuter from the big town of Dover and it is generally considered that when he cannot be found in Durham one can find him on Park St. in Dover. The reason for his presence on Park Street is a Dolly in the window. Tom has advanced beyond the would-be chemist state by having worked for a dye corporation determining the moisture content of the hot air used in the selling of the dyes, and other things. This year Tom was initiated into the Alpha Chi Sigma and no doubt will some day rank with the other inert chemical elements.

name: Thompson, Katharine
motto: Grow old along with me, the best is yet to be, The last of life for which the first was made.
description: Tommy couldn't even wait to get to college before acquiring en route one of those guys with a college-bred air but since then the prevailing air has been from quite a different direction. But it is futile to continue the long list of Tommy's masculine acquisitions and the puns thereto attached. Besides, as Tommy herself is so fond of saying, "A pun is the lowest form of humor." She decided that when she changed her major to English. That was her sophomore year. This year has been remarkable for that change of political sentiment which she touchingly refers to as her conversion and that other distracting interest known as reversion to type.

motto: He may be small, but, oh! how he can lo--.
description: "Mert" isn't much of an athlete except in one sport, parlor rugby. In this he plays a stellar role. All the girls in the metropolis of Newfields fall for him but "stenogs" are much more appealing to his whims. One morning the above fairfaced youngster thought he would enter the pugilistic hall of fame. Accordingly he begins on the "brakie" of the Newfields Limited. Since then "Mert" believes that engineering is much more profitable from a physical standpoint.

name: Tingley, Edythe
motto: And the wonder is that one small head could hold all she knows.
description: "Tyng" sharks "chem," "zoo," and everything else. Her knowledge causes us ordinary mortals to gasp with amazement. In spite of the fact that she looks sober and studious she can appreciate a joke and has quite a sense of humor. Studying is her vice — athletics is her recreation — hiking and camping are her hobbies. You are fortunate if you can go camping with "Tyng." Can she cook? I'll say she can! She intends to devote her capable self to teaching. If Tyng has anything to do about it they will be students.

name: Thayer, Royal Goodell
motto: Drink to me only with thine eyes.
description: "Satch" came from the Lee side of Cushing Academy with the ambition to be a farmer but his present ambition seems to be to flunk out — and to make the "long splice." He was the anchor man on the class basketball team — anchored to the floor. He tried once to be the house manager but failed to find magnetic instruments enough to locate the furnace. "Satch" seems especially quiet to all outsiders save one, and apparently she has thrown a stone into the placid waters of his calm existence. At present he's "Napoleon Gomo" from "Vermont."

Drink to me only with thine eyes.
"Satch" came from the Lee side of Cushing Academy with the ambition to be a farmer but his present ambition seems to be to flunk out — and to make the "long splice." He was the anchor man on the class basketball team — anchored to the floor. He tried once to be the house manager but failed to find magnetic instruments enough to locate the furnace. "Satch" seems especially quiet to all outsiders save one, and apparently she has thrown a stone into the placid waters of his calm existence. At present he's "Napoleon Gomo" from "Vermont."

name: True, Laurence Melburne
motto: Did I ever tell you?
description: About seven times a day this fellow calls around and springs a new old one. His one original joke came near being a tragedy when "Poodle" tried to go through the door by the window and at that in the wrong way. He has a wonderful store of knowledge about spark plugs, and carburetors, and Studebakers, and we sometimes wonder where he keeps it concealed behind his mask of studious appearance. One would imagine him to be an historian or a "soci" shark rather than an affable chauffeur.

name: Turner, Howard Edmundson
motto: To those who know thee not, no words can paint; And those who know thee, know all words are faint.
description: Ed says that an Engineering Course is incomplete without Library class, so he has been delving into the mysteries of Economics. And his little Corona types sheet upon sheet of notes, yea, even unto the early hours of the morning—long after the wise Engineers are in bed. There is an irresistible force attracting Ed toward Salem every so often or oftener. "Oh the wild, wild women." You can rely upon Ed's getting those E. E. reports in before anyone else does as he is taking no chances. If you want to see Ed's heels just light up a cigarette.

name: Vance, Arlo McCrillis
motto: Drink to me only with thine eyes, And I will pledge with mine. Or leave a kiss but in the cup, And I'll not look for wine.
description: Not to insinuate that "Vancie" cares for wine—but we are not saying a word about the fair sex! Arlo came to us in 1919 fresh from Gordon Bible College and many wonderful experiences in the Canadian West. Now he has turned to the more prosaic but never-the-less sometimes alarming study of "bugs," human and otherwise. In spite of the bugs, Arlo has considerable business instinct which he shows in divers ways. But for all of that he is a "damned" good fellow.

name: Warner, Norwood Allan
motto: The biggest noise at the Commons.
description: Just to introduce this blue-eyed, black haired boy, a specimen from the cranberry bogs of Cape Cod. A typical engineer, believing in the laws of the Conservation of Energy, free love and the Fourth dimension. He is a charter member of the back seat gang of the Dean's "Mechanics" class. Since the old Mess Hall days he has been a faithful servant of the college in the department of eats. Many of the old gang have weakened but "Sodium" still breaks the dishes and can be seen in action any morning between the hours of 7.45 and 8.00 a.m. Military Art beckoned to him and he is passing away many hours at this vocation. His many friends hope that some day he will be a great General—Nuisance.

name: Watkins, Robert Eldridge
motto: What he knows not, is not worth knowing.
description: Some think that Bob is a fair hash slinger, but others prefer to think of him as one of those exceptional bull throwers. Bob tells us you can't kid him for his best girl comes from Missouri. (Fulton.)

name: Weston, George Washington
motto: The way to have a friend is to be one.
description: The gentleman from Wilton was christened "Skip", — short for "Skipper" — because he never does — and although he may be a barbarous and woolly cave man he is not Ruth-less. He wants to remember however, that those window shades in Ballard Hall were there for a reason. Skip is a firm believer in the sanctity of home ties and says he is going to stick to Mother — Mother Earth. He is slowly and painfully but surely metamorphosing into a real agriculturalist and we all wish him success.

name: Whitney, Orrin Calvin
motto: Sweet Durham, loveliest village of the plain.
description: "Whit" is another man who decided that the class of 1922 was better than its predecessors. So during his sophomore year he went home to see the girl once more and to enjoy all the social functions of the old town — and all this just to be with us this year and get his picture in the 1922 GRANITE. "Whit" is now congratulating himself on his choice of courses and he says that the "Aggie" men are going to have a wonderful time after the departure of the Arts and Science "drones." Although "Orrin's" failing may not be women, don't fool yourself — you never can tell by the looks.

name: Whittier, Helen
motto: Here's to the girl who can dance and play and represents us in Y. W. C. A. Her taste in literature may not be the best, but when it comes to a zoology test, she's there! Here's to the girl who's good in gym; on every team she's out to win. Full of pep, and keen in thought, all in all we hand her a lot. Here's to Whit!

name: Wiggin, Russell Morrison
description: When the S. A. T. C. disbanded, Doc found himself the possessor of a cotton uniform and an aristocratic walk befitting a general. He has discarded the uniform but not the walk. Doc is a member of the second floor gang of Fairchild, and it is rumored that he is so noisy that three of his former roommates have decided to leave college after rooming with him a short while. Doc never gets enthusiastic except when telling about spending his summers watching the moon rise in a certain part of the White Mountains, but he never states why he finds this such a pleasant pastime.

name: Wood, Harold Barnette
motto: Do you want to go on a good party?
clubs: Second Lieutenant, R. O. T. C. (2)
description: This apparently meek youth, whose rosy cheeks remind one that Palmolive soap is the best for one's complexion, hails from the historic city of Portsmouth. He came to New Hampshire College as an "engineer", and by persistent burning of the midnight oil still maintains that name. But "Deke" can hear the waves of the sea of Arts and Science calling him louder and louder at the end of each term, and besides Harold has a constant longing for the warmth of the Library. It is rumored that "Deke" has a strong craving for "Rye", but his only comment is "Oh (Hell) en."

name: Woodward, Bert Arthur
motto: "If you can't stay long go often;" "The course of true love never runs smooth."
description: Hark! 1-2-3-4-5-6-7-8-9-9:30. Alas, 'tis thou must leave, Bertie. And Bertie went! But one night Bertie over-stayed. And in the wee sma' hours of the morn was heard —! xx! **—! —!! XX XX!! crank,—crank, — *x*x! — !! crank,— crank. Bertie, always should thou kick off the switch, or indeed thou shalt ride home in thy 1912 Overland by Ford power. Moral: Thy auto ever needs a spark. Bertie came to college with a deep affection for the engineering course but at the end of the first term discovered that the feeling was far from mutual; alas, he deteriorated to a man of (many) letters.

name: Yeaton, George Samuel
motto: "Oh, how I hate to get up in the morning."
description: "Sam" first struck Durham on the first of January 1919. We wonder why he came here? The first two years Sam was homesick, but this year things have changed. Sam is a loyal patron of the cafeteria, but somehow or other he always manages to eat after the crowd. Sam isn't alone by any means, but he brings his "seraglio" with him and enjoys a good long meal. Sam's chief ambition is to cut classes for he thinks that is part of the course.

name: Young, Esther Hale
motto: "She never wrongs her neighbor And is prompt to help a friend And has those stern qualities of true womanhood."
description: If you are ever in trouble,— call on Esther. She will readily lend a helping hand to aid you through some unknown passage in French or that mysterious paragraph in Psychology. Esther is a sincere and conscientious worker in her studies and makes good in whatever she undertakes. Oh those class notes, Esther, which you have taken and shared, have saved many a feeble brain from worry! Esther's ever ready smile to greet you at once testifies her loyalty, and surely we cannot help but appreciate such a true and steady friend.
